title: Loop Spec — the neutral loop spec (loop-forge)
description: The single type the classifier, interviewer, assembler, and renderers hand around. Platform-neutral JSON. The loop-registry's 5 cells are first-class under `registry`, plus the language-independent `external_gate` enum. 8 scalar fields + the registry object = all 9 fields required; additionalProperties:false guards against typos.

name.description: The slash-command name (slug). Starts lowercase, then lowercase/digits/hyphens, 2-31 chars total. e.g. review-summary

archetype.description: One of the 5 loop shapes. Never invent a 6th shape (YAGNI).

label.description: The shape's canonical English label. Values: Batch / Pipeline / Refine / Watch / Explore (rendered in the user's language at stamp time).

situation.description: The user's original one-line situation, verbatim and in any language (e.g. I want to summarize 100 shop reviews into 3 lines each).

scope.description: Where the stamp is saved. project=the current project's .claude/commands, global=~/.claude/commands. Chosen by the user in Stage 6.

slots.description: Per-shape slot key=value. Keys are defined by that archetype.md catalog (e.g. batch=input_source/per_item_task/output_format/max_items). Values follow the slot definition (string, number, array of strings, etc.).

skeleton.description: The procedure body with slot variables substituted. The assembler fills the shape skeleton's {slot} placeholders with the user's answers.

external_action.description: Whether it sends/posts outbound. true raises the outbound-gate bar (dry-run/human approval first). Watch defaults to true.

registry.description: The loop-registry's 5 cells + the language-independent external_gate enum. Every cell is a non-empty string (minLength>=1). The assembler auto-injects the shape defaults.

trigger.description: How the loop fires. Default = manual /command run (Watch = condition/interval).

gate.description: The entry/stage-pass condition (e.g. batch = N <= max_items).

verifier.description: The inspector (maker != checker). The assembler auto-injects the shape's default verifier text. For watch/external_action, the verifier prose describes the outbound gate (dry-run / human approval).

hardstop.description: The stop switch (budget/count/cooldown). The assembler auto-injects the shape's default hardstop text.

accepted_signal.description: The observation that counts as done (e.g. N rows present in the output + a failure-log count).

external_gate.description: The language-independent outbound gate. If archetype==watch or external_action==true, must be dry_run or human_approval (never none). check_safety.py enforces on this enum, not on prose.
